doc_title: Quantum Annealers Chain Strengths: A Simple Heuristic to Set Them All

items.evidence_span: The idea of using Quantum Annealers to solve combinatorial problems is not new and was exposed by E. Farhi et al. 9.

It comes from the fact that quantum annealers implement a noisy version of the more general Adiabatic Quantum Computation (AQC) (the reader may refer to T. Albash et al. [2] for an introduction to AQC).
Indeed, D-Wave systems suffer from more than five different sources of Integrated Control Errors (ICE) 1].
As mentioned in V. Gilbert et al. 10, assessing the quality of an embedding is not trivial.
To the authors' knowledge, a single contribution on this issue was made by E. Pelofske 13 .

items.fact: V. Choi also details a more elaborated bound calculated in O(D L)
items.evidence_span: V. Choi also details a more elaborated bound calculated in O(D L)

items.fact: In the paper of Fang et al. 8
items.evidence_span: In the paper of Fang et al. 8

items.fact: The authors of 18 provided a second approach
items.evidence_span: The authors of 18 provided a second approach

items.fact: In the paper of Raymond et al. [15]
items.evidence_span: In the paper of Raymond et al. [15]

items.fact: This paper led to the default method uniform_torque_compensation implemented on D-Wave systems 11
entities: default method uniform_torque_compensation implemented on D-Wave systems
items.evidence_span: This paper led to the default method uniform_torque_compensation implemented on D-Wave systems 11

items.fact: For detailed experimental studies on chain strength scanning, the reader may refer to [12]19
items.evidence_span: For detailed experimental studies on chain strength scanning, the reader may refer to [12]19

items.fact: A single advanced optimization method of the chain strength has been developed for the max-clique problem by H. Djidjev recently and is based on augmented lagrangian method 7
items.evidence_span: A single advanced optimization method of the chain strength has been developed for the max-clique problem by H. Djidjev recently and is based on augmented lagrangian method 7

items.fact: In another recent paper of Pelofske [14]
items.evidence_span: In another recent paper of Pelofske [14]

items.fact: Choi's first bound is used to set the default interval's upper bound
items.evidence_span: the default interval's upper bound is set according to Choi's first bound (line 3)
